spec_match.rationale: Each preset emits the canonical `<script type="permissionconstraints">` block plus the equivalent `getUserMedia()` argument — the spec's two surfaces, side by side.

interactive.rationale: Six preset cards + `Apply & probe` button request a real stream and surface `track.getSettings()` to show what the browser actually negotiated.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a preset list with the constraints block, the JS equivalent, and a rationale table. All three render.

edge_cases.rationale: Six presets is a reasonable surface, but no preset exercises `OverconstrainedError` — a preset called `Impossible Constraints` would teach more than another happy path.

design_system.rationale: Variables-only throughout, but one stray `✓` glyph; convention is inline SVG.

openQuestions.title: Add chromestatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: see-also lists only siblings; the chromestatus.com/feature/4926233538330624 anchor is required by the self-heal invariant.

openQuestions.title: Add a failing-constraint preset
openQuestions.detail: All six presets succeed on common hardware. Add an `8K Cinema` preset that the browser must reject so visitors see the OverconstrainedError path. That is where the negotiated-settings table earns its keep.

openQuestions.title: Show the gap between requested and negotiated
openQuestions.detail: When the browser downgrades a request, the negotiated `track.getSettings()` differs from the constraint. Diff-highlight the negotiated panel so the deltas are visible at a glance.

openQuestions.title: Replace ✓ with inline SVG
openQuestions.detail: One unicode checkmark in the negotiated panel; project rule is inline SVG.

summary: Six concrete starting points plus a live probe — a useful pattern. Add the chromestatus link, add a failing preset for OverconstrainedError, and the page becomes both a cheat-sheet and a constraint-negotiation lesson.
